---
title: Munich Airport Traffic
---

Munich Airport (MUC) is one of Europe's busiest hubs. The
[Monatszahlen Flugverkehr](https://opendata.muenchen.de/dataset/monatszahlen-flugverkehr)
dataset, published monthly by the Statistisches Amt München, records passenger
volumes and flight movements back to 1992.

This demo applies the same pipeline pattern as the cycling counters: the portal
publishes a single full-history CSV that is replaced each month, so we download
it into a date-stamped file, validate with
[`@schema`](../reference/schema.schema.qmd), and upsert into a Delta Lake table
using [`@incremental`](../reference/incremental.incremental.qmd). Upsert on the
natural key `(MONATSZAHL, AUSPRAEGUNG, JAHR, MONAT)` ensures that revised
figures are corrected automatically without duplicates.

**Data source:** Statistisches Amt München ·
License: [Datenlizenz Deutschland – Namensnennung 2.0](https://www.govdata.de/dl-de/by-2-0)

```{python}
#| echo: false
try:
    from dotenv import load_dotenv
    load_dotenv()
except ImportError:
    print("python-dotenv not found, skipping .env loading") 
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
import os
from pathlib import Path

import altair as alt
import polars as pl

BUCKET = os.environ.get("MUNICH_CYCLING_BUCKET", "data-warehousing-with-polars")
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
df = (
    pl.scan_delta(f"s3://{BUCKET}/delta/munich_airport")
    .select("MONATSZAHL", "AUSPRAEGUNG", "JAHR", "WERT", "date")
    .collect()
    .sort("date")
)
df.head(5)
```

## Monthly passenger volume

Total passengers (boarding + alighting + transit) per month since 1992. The
COVID-19 collapse in 2020 and subsequent recovery are unmistakable.

```{python}
#| echo: false
passengers = (
    df.filter(
        (pl.col("MONATSZAHL") == "Fluggäste")
        & (pl.col("AUSPRAEGUNG") == "insgesamt")
    )
    .drop_nulls("WERT")
)

alt.Chart(passengers).mark_area(
    line={"color": "#4c78a8"},
    color=alt.Gradient(
        gradient="linear",
        stops=[
            alt.GradientStop(color="rgba(76,120,168,0.6)", offset=0),
            alt.GradientStop(color="rgba(76,120,168,0.05)", offset=1),
        ],
        x1=1, x2=1, y1=1, y2=0,
    ),
).encode(
    x=alt.X("date:T", title="Month"),
    y=alt.Y("WERT:Q", title="Passengers", axis=alt.Axis(format="~s")),
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip("date:T", title="Month", format="%b %Y"),
        alt.Tooltip("WERT:Q", title="Passengers", format=",.0f"),
    ],
).properties(
    width="container", height=260, title="Munich Airport — total passengers per month"
).configure_view(strokeWidth=0)
```

## Passenger breakdown

Departing, arriving, and transit passengers plotted separately, with an
interactive legend to show/hide categories.

```{python}
#| echo: false
breakdown = (
    df.filter(
        (pl.col("MONATSZAHL") == "Fluggäste")
        & (pl.col("AUSPRAEGUNG") != "insgesamt")
    )
    .drop_nulls("WERT")
)

color_scale = alt.Scale(
    domain=["Einsteigende", "Aussteigende", "Transit"],
    range=["#4c78a8", "#72b7b2", "#f58518"],
)

selection = alt.selection_point(fields=["AUSPRAEGUNG"], bind="legend")

alt.Chart(breakdown).mark_line(strokeWidth=1.5).encode(
    x=alt.X("date:T", title="Month"),
    y=alt.Y("WERT:Q", title="Passengers", axis=alt.Axis(format="~s")),
    color=alt.Color(
        "AUSPRAEGUNG:N",
        scale=color_scale,
        legend=alt.Legend(title="Direction"),
    ),
    opacity=alt.condition(selection, alt.value(1), alt.value(0.1)),
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip("date:T", title="Month", format="%b %Y"),
        alt.Tooltip("AUSPRAEGUNG:N", title="Direction"),
        alt.Tooltip("WERT:Q", title="Passengers", format=",.0f"),
    ],
).add_params(selection).properties(
    width="container", height=260, title="Passengers by direction"
).configure_view(strokeWidth=0)
```

## Flight movements

Total take-offs and landings per month (passenger flights vs cargo/post),
showing the airport's traffic composition over time.

```{python}
#| echo: false
movements = (
    df.filter(
        (pl.col("MONATSZAHL") == "Flugzeugbewegungen")
        & (pl.col("AUSPRAEGUNG").is_in(["Passagierflüge", "Fracht-/Postflüge"]))
    )
    .drop_nulls("WERT")
)

color_scale2 = alt.Scale(
    domain=["Passagierflüge", "Fracht-/Postflüge"],
    range=["#4c78a8", "#e45756"],
)

selection2 = alt.selection_point(fields=["AUSPRAEGUNG"], bind="legend")

alt.Chart(movements).mark_line(strokeWidth=1.5).encode(
    x=alt.X("date:T", title="Month"),
    y=alt.Y("WERT:Q", title="Flight movements", axis=alt.Axis(format="~s")),
    color=alt.Color(
        "AUSPRAEGUNG:N",
        scale=color_scale2,
        legend=alt.Legend(title="Flight type"),
    ),
    opacity=alt.condition(selection2, alt.value(1), alt.value(0.1)),
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip("date:T", title="Month", format="%b %Y"),
        alt.Tooltip("AUSPRAEGUNG:N", title="Type"),
        alt.Tooltip("WERT:Q", title="Movements", format=",.0f"),
    ],
).add_params(selection2).properties(
    width="container", height=240, title="Flight movements by type"
).configure_view(strokeWidth=0)
```

## Seasonality

Average monthly passengers (all years), confirming a strong summer peak and a
trough in January–February — the pattern COVID disrupted then re-established.

```{python}
#| echo: false
seasons = (
    passengers
    .with_columns(
        pl.col("date").dt.month().alias("month_num"),
        pl.col("date").dt.strftime("%b").alias("month_name"),
    )
    .group_by("month_num", "month_name")
    .agg(pl.col("WERT").mean().alias("avg_passengers"))
    .sort("month_num")
)

alt.Chart(seasons).mark_bar(color="#4c78a8").encode(
    x=alt.X("month_name:N", sort=None, title="Month"),
    y=alt.Y(
        "avg_passengers:Q",
        title="Avg passengers",
        axis=alt.Axis(format="~s"),
    ),
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip("month_name:N", title="Month"),
        alt.Tooltip("avg_passengers:Q", title="Avg passengers", format=",.0f"),
    ],
).properties(
    width="container", height=220, title="Average passengers by month of year"
).configure_view(strokeWidth=0)
```
